summary: Vals/vals missing from BSONObject
description: Vals/vals methods are missing from BSONObject but present in documentation and also mentioned in  bsonelement.h comments.

summary: It must be OK to run the destructors for static objects created by the C++ driver
description: As part of SERVER-5520, we're moving away form executing static destructors in mongo programs.  C++ client users will still presumably be calling exit() or returning from main(), and so running their destructors.  We'll want to test that this keeps working.

summary: Remove StaticObserver and all uses in ReplicaSetMonitor
description: Fragile ordering of calls to destructors of static objects have been causing intermittent test failures. The best way to get rid of this behavior is to just rid ourself of fragile ordering guarantees in creation/destruction of static objects.

summary: Initialization Problem with C++11/14
C++ "Examples" doc is incomplete
description:
The docs/nav for 0.11.0 and 1.0.0 include an examples page:
 http://api.mongodb.org/cxx/legacy-0.11.0/examples.html
 http://api.mongodb.org/cxx/legacy-1.0.0-rc0/examples.html

In both cases the "using" example is incomplete:
 http://api.mongodb.org/cxx/legacy-1.0.0-rc0/_using-example.html

Not sure if that is a doxygen fail, but these pages aren't useful at the moment.

summary: Cannot use shutdown at exit feature on Windows
Execution of unit tests should not spawn mongod
description: Currently, a mongod is spawned before unit tests are executed. As unit tests are intended to not require a server, this step should not be performed.

summary: Interactions with mongo-orchestration use removed 'uri' field
description: Mongo-orchestration's master has removed support for the 'uri' field that we parse the port number of the mongo process out of. As Travis-CI uses mongo-orchestration's master this breaks all Travis builds.

summary: Windows builds missing include and lib paths for curl
Disable ParallelCollectionScanBadConnections on non-mmap storage engines
description: The semantics this test probes appears to be somewhat more relaxed on non-mmapv1 storage engines. As such, it should be disabled for them.

summary: Client tests can pass, when they can't possibly pass
description: Success is dependent on the OR of all returns. If any pass, the suite passes. These codes should be ANDed instead.

summary: Integration shouldn't test storage engine specific collstats field "userFlags"
description: "userFlags" is not provided by the heap1 storage engine. We should not rely upon its existence.

summary: Remove use of getIndexes in tests on post 2.6 servers
description: Use of the systems index collection is deprecated, and generally not supported by non-mmapv1 storage engines. As such, invocations of raw getIndexes should be removed from the tests.

summary: DBClientWithCommands::reIndex should not use getSystemIndexesCollection
description: reIndex currently accumulates a list of indexes, deletes them, then reinserts them into the system index collection. We should not use the system index collection. Where possible, we should use the reIndex command in the database.

summary: Do not rely on structure in the natural ordering in clientTest
description:
According to http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/method/cursor.sort/#return-natural-order:

"The $natural parameter returns items according to their natural order within the database. This ordering is an internal implementation feature, and you should not rely on any particular structure within it."

Remove test assert which makes assumptions on the document returned by findOne({})

summary: Do not use MONGODB-CR for recent 2.7 builds
description: Recent versions of mongod will automatically create new users with SCRAM credentials. Trying to authenticate with them using MONGODB-CR will fail. This results in a failure in the auth client tests.

summary: SCRAM-SHA-1 should be used in  when max wire version is greater than or equal to 3
description:
Under wire version 3, the current wire version, servers have two operation modes. If it's a partly upgraded server, with users created with MONGODB-CR, it will operate in a compatibility mode which, with some overhead, will allow users to connect using SCRAM to those accounts. If the server has been completely upgraded, and all users have been migrated to native SCRAM, or the server has been freshly brought up on 2.8, then it will only accept incoming authentication requests using SCRAM. MONGODB-CR requests will be denied.

Currently, we only use SCRAM when the max wire version of the server is greater than 3. This means we will currently always use MONGODB-CR when connecting to a 2.8 server. If the user has not manually specified an authentication mechanism, as in the auth method which accepts the username and password as strings, we should default to using SCRAM for wire version >= 3, and allow the server to sort out how to handle it.

summary: SCRAM-SHA-1 should be usable with only native sasl
description: It should be possible to compile the driver with --ssl, and without --use-sasl-client, and be able to authenticate to a mongod using SCRAM-SHA1. The driver should, if it is not so compiled, provide an error message which explains the flag needed.

summary: DBClient should inform user that --ssl is needed for SCRAM-SHA-1
description: When performing auth, if the driver was not compiled with --ssl it is not possible to authenticate using SCRAM-SHA-1. If the user attempts to authenticate with the mechanism without this flag, when authentication fails, we should produce an error message which indicates that this is the underlying problem.

summary: Change name of heap1 to inMemoryExperiment
description: The name of this storage engine has changed.

summary: Windows MongoOrchestration tests fail
description: https://mci.10gen.com/task/mongodb_cpp_driver_windows_64_msvc2010_static_static_8422c6110f141bc8cb7d0b19f1c11280ab91cbd1_14_11_06_17_41_06_integration_test_latest_windows_64_msvc2010_static_static

summary: Integration test that SCRAM is the default auth mechanism for wire version 3
description: SCRAM-SHA-1 should be the auth mechanism used for when the max wire version is 3 or higher. Test this in the integration suite.

summary: ConnectionString should default to using the specified DB and use a default mechanism
description:
Connecting with ConnectionString works great when not authenticating.

Authentication needs implicit authMechanism authSource declarations.

I propose using the specified DB (if specified and authSource isn't) as the connection string already allows for this:
mongodb://user:pass@host:port/DB

Furthermore authMechanism has no default value and must be explicitly defined in the connection string.

summary: clang 3.5 warning, dbclientcursor.h
description:
Using the clang 3.5 compiler, the following warning is issued during compilation of the 26compat branch.

While this may not be of immediate concern, it seems odd enough to report.

clang++ -O3 -g -std=c++11 -stdlib=libc++ -I ../libs/ami/include -emit-llvm -c 
In file included from ../libs/ami/include/mongo/client/dbclient.h:40:
In file included from ../libs/ami/include/mongo/client/connpool.h:22:
In file included from ../libs/ami/include/mongo/client/dbclientinterface.h:1374:
../libs/ami/include/mongo/client/dbclientcursor.h:120:40: warning: 'this' pointer cannot be null in well-defined C++ code; pointer may be
      assumed to always convert to true [-Wundefined-bool-conversion]
        bool isDead() const { return  !this || cursorId == 0; }
                                      ~^~~~
1 warning generated.

summary: client examples should take both host and port arguments, not just port
description: We should not require that the local system be able to run mongod in order to run the drivers examples.

description: Make sure mongo-orchestration doesn't create temporary files in /tmp.

Support cursor in listIndexes command
Support cursor in listCollections command
Expose rich field information in the updateDescription
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2344}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Add option for nightly mongoDB to drivers-evergreen-tool
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

_Right now drivers-evergreen-tool will download 'latest' and test with that, however this is the latest release candidate, which can be behind master. We would like the option to run drivers-evergreen-tool with a passed in binary._

_The tool currently will always download a specified version of mongoDB, however we would like to feature that if we provide a binary and place it in the pathing of MONGODB_BINARY, that it would use this binary instead of trying to download one._
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

_Teams using this tool will be able to run orchestrations using specific mongoDB binaries that the user can provide._
h4. How does this affect the end user?

_allow for more testing options by the user._
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

_N/A_
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

_N/A_
h4. Is this issue urgent?

_This issue currently blocks our projects testing._
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

_Our team creating Mongotune currently uses drivers-evergreen-tool for our integration tests._
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

_No_
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

_Allow drivers-evergreen-tool to pass in a mongodb binary of the user's choice for orchestration to then run on._

_When passing a binary, say by using the MONGODB_BINARY path and possible a flag, drivers-evergreen-tool would not download the default MONGODB_VERSION and instead use the binary provided on the path._
{panel}

summary: Support github contributing guidelines
description: See https://github.com/blog/1184-contributing-guidelines

summary: Handle server command responses without "ok" field
description: According to SERVER-10891, commands don't return "ok" field if targeting databases with invalid characters. (Essentially, the server is failing to execute a query on "$cmd", and it hasn't yet entered the command-processing path.) Drivers must expect a command response document without an "ok" field, in which case "$err" will be set.

summary: Proper handling of RECOVERING nodes
Mongo-Orchestration Needs to Support Windows
description:
After looking into this more closely, it appears that it _kinda_ works on windows.  

After editing the mongo-orchestration.config file, you can start the restful server using {code}python server.py --no-fork{code}.  Using this without --no-fork causes some issues related to spinning up a separate process and I haven't worked through that yet.

Once this is up, you can run {code}python helpers\cmd_hosts.py{code}.  This will put you into a little repl in which you can do a couple of things.
# create {"name": "mongod"} will appear to fail, but really do what it is supposed to do
# info 1 will provide back the information that should have come back from the create call
# start 1 will start the guy you created
# stop 1 will appear to fail, but does actually stop the server

I haven't done anything with replica sets or shards.

summary: drivers should SO_LINGER when closing sockets
description:
suppose my program does some writes and immediately then closes the connection and terminates.  do my writes get there?

generally they will, if SO_LINGER is specified - but see article below for caveats.  so

1) should we use SO_LINGER in all drivers? i think so?
2) in addition, if last operation on a connection was a write, should we automatically call getlasterror on connection destruction?

re:#2, even if the data gets there, the GLE call can be argued for as my expectation might be that my program does not terminate until the data is delivered and applied.

* http://blog.netherlabs.nl/articles/2009/01/18/the-ultimate-so_linger-page-or-why-is-my-tcp-not-reliable

summary: gridfs chunksize should be lowered to 255K
description: Drivers should lower chunk size to 255K (from 256K).

summary: Implement default write acknowledgement with new MongoClient class
zstd cause memory leak
description:
h3. Detailed steps to reproduce the problem?

_create a simple app  that use zstd as Compressors_

 
{code:java}
    ClientOptions := options.Client().ApplyURI(str).SetCompressors([]string{"zstd"}).SetBSONOptions(bsonOpts).SetServerSelectionTimeout(30 * time.Second)
    var err error
    config.Client, err = mongo.Connect(context.Background(), ClientOptions)
    
        collection := config.Client.Database(config.MainConf.Main_db).Collection("flexyoperations")
    changeStream, errChangeStream := collection.Watch(context.Background(), mongo.Pipeline{
        bson.D{{Key: "$match", Value: bson.M{"operationType": "insert"}}},
    }) {code}
 

_a simple function that save a struct_ 

 
{code:java}
func SaveServer() {
	mutexSave.Lock()
	defer mutexSave.Unlock()

	server.LastSeen = time.Now()
	logger.DBL.Info("DB Server Save => Called ", zap.Any("server", server))
	if config.Client == nil {
		ServerStatus.Set("Pending")
		dialog.ShowError(errors.New("not Connected"), config.Window)
		logger.DBL.Error("DB Server Load => Client is nil ")
		return
	}
	_, err := config.Client.Database(config.MainConf.Main_db).Collection("flexyservers").UpdateOne(context.TODO(), bson.M{"server_name": config.MainConf.Server_name},
		bson.M{"$set": server})
	if err != nil {
		logger.DBL.Error("DB Server SaveServer => Error: ", zap.Any("err", err))
		return
	}
} {code}
h3. just after calling saveserver 5- 10 time i can see huge memory usage , it's can go up to 2.5gb after my app run for few hours when i start debuging using pprof i can see the following just calling save few times

 
{code:java}
      flat  flat%   sum%        cum   cum%
     256MB 77.50% 77.50%      256MB 77.50%  github.com/klauspost/compress/zstd.(*fastBase).ensureHist (inline)
   48.86MB 14.79% 92.29%    48.86MB 14.79%  github.com/klauspost/compress/zstd.encoderOptions.encoder
   10.99MB  3.33% 95.62%    10.99MB  3.33%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/loader.(*Loader).findTableBuffer
    2.26MB  0.68% 96.31%     2.26MB  0.68%  github.com/klauspost/compress/zstd.(*blockEnc).init
       2MB  0.61% 96.91%        4MB  1.21%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/tables.(*Glyph).parseData
         0     0% 96.91%       32MB  9.69%  flexyserver/Repositories.LoadOffers
         0     0% 96.91%    32.57MB  9.86%  flexyserver/Repositories.LoadServer
         0     0% 96.91%   161.70MB 48.95%  flexyserver/Repositories.SaveServer
         0     0% 96.91%    81.36MB 24.63%  flexyserver/services/db.Connect
         0     0% 96.91%    11.91MB  3.61%  flexyserver/ui.ShowMainUI {code}
simply changing from zstd to snappy or zlib resolve my issue and my app doesn't exceed 150mb memory usage

 
{code:java}
      flat  flat%   sum%        cum   cum%
13932.58kB 38.75% 38.75% 13932.58kB 38.75%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/loader.(*Loader).findTableBuffer
 3584.50kB  9.97% 48.72%  3584.50kB  9.97%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/tables.(*SimpleGlyph).parsePoints
 2707.76kB  7.53% 56.26%  2707.76kB  7.53%  compress/flate.NewWriter
 2560.18kB  7.12% 63.38%  6656.70kB 18.52%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/tables.(*Glyph).parseData
 2384.64kB  6.63% 70.01%  2384.64kB  6.63%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/api/font/cff.(*cffParser).parseIndex
 1131.52kB  3.15% 73.16%  7788.22kB 21.66%  github.com/go-text/typesetting/opentype/tables.ParseGlyf
  632.14kB  1.76% 74.92%  1144.27kB  3.18%  fyne.io/fyne/v2/internal/cache.SetCanvasForObject
  600.58kB  1.67% 76.59%   600.58kB  1.67%  github.com/go-playground/validator/v10.map.init.4
  574.34kB  1.60% 78.18%   574.34kB  1.60%  flexyserver/services/serviceat.(*ATService).readUntilEmpty
  570.04kB  1.59% 79.77% 17542.25kB 48.79%  fyne.io/fyne/v2/internal/cache.Renderer {code}
h3.  
h3. Definition of done: what must be done to consider the task complete?
h3. i created an issue to 
https://github.com/klauspost/compress/issues/928 
h3. he said the issue is with the mongodb client library and not with his library 


h3. The exact Go version used, with patch level:

{{$ go version}}

go version go1.21.1 linux/amd64
h3. The exact version of the Go driver used:

{{$ go list -m go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver}}

mongodb server local ( replicaset )

summary: Fix `server-selection/tests/logging/operation-id.json:Failed client bulkWrite operation: log messages have operationIds`
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2978}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: all method that take a DBObject, make a version that takes a map
description:
There are many places in the Mongo Java API that use the DBObject interface as parameter or return value.  BasicDBObject is the most commonly used implementation of DBObject, which implements java.util.Map, but since none of the methods accept or return Map, we're forced to make intermediate BasicDBObject wrappers around objects that could otherwise be passed straight in.  If these methods were updated to accept/return Maps, usage could be simpler and less onerous on user code.  

Below is a list of the spots in the API that could benefit (I think) from being updated:

The DBObject interface could implement Map
BasicDBObject - constructor could accept a Map
DBCursor.toArray() - could return List<Map>
DBCursor.curr(), explain(), next() could all return Map
DBCollection - ensureIndex() and friends could accept Map
DBCollection - find() & findOne() could accept Map
DBCollection - getIndexInfo() could return List<Map>
DBCollection - apply(), insert(), remove(), save(), update() could all accept Map

summary: connection management should take available memory into account
description: It's possible to run into Out Of Memory errors pretty quickly when a Mongo() instance creates multiple connections to the DB and the JVM has been invoked without specifying enough memory.  The connection management should ideally take into account the available memory when allocating new connections.  In a debug or verbose mode, the driver might indicate that it would have liked to create more connections but couldn't due to memory restrictions.

summary: make ObjectId string rep same as dbshell?
Expose putAll(Map) method to DBObject
description: For allowing clients to put in multiple entries (from an existing Map) into a (Basic)DBObject with a single call, it would be great if you can expose the method putAll (inherited by java.util.Map), this would allow to write more straightforward code when transferring entries from an already existing map,

summary: Could JSON#serialize support DBRef?
description: com.mongodb.BasicDBObject#toString throws RuntimeException if that contains DBRef.

summary: DBCollection.update method javadoc comment points to an old 10gen page
description:
It should point to:

http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/Atomic+Operations

summary: JSON.parse() should work with \uXXXX
Method/param semantic issues
description:
In most documentation, you seem to refer to the attributes of a document as fields. In the Java driver documentation, you refer to them as keys (probably because you are using a map).

The following methods use two different terms:

containsKey(key)
removeField(key)

The rest of the methods refer to keys. This should be consistent with the rest of the documentation. If they are fields then set the param name as "name" or "fieldName". If you settle on fields then I would deprecate containsKey and add containsField(name).
